response.justification: The candidate brings genuinely relevant geospatial/GIS depth and demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching, microservices handling millions of requests/second), which aligns well with the routing-pipeline domain and distributed-systems requirements. However, the core hard requirement—modern C++ or equivalent Rust experience—is weak: the only C/C++ work is older NI LabWindows/CVI and a bachelor thesis, with no recent commercial modern-C++ and no Rust at all. Most recent and primary experience is in TypeScript/Node and DLang, not the target stack. A maybe, pending verification of current C++/systems-programming proficiency.
strengths: Strong geospatial/GIS background (multiple mapping platforms, FOSS4G talk)
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching, microservices)
Linux, distributed/microservices experience and strong engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust commercial experience; C/C++ work is dated
Primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node and DLang, not the required C++/Rust
